questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with respect to
Web 4.0 Services:
I. - It will aim at the interaction between humans
and machines in symbiosis.
II. 	 In the Web 4.0 world, there can be the
development of Virtual Reality (VR) and
Augmented Reality (AR) applications.
III. 	 Web 4.0 services enable users to access
tailored content based on their interests and
preferences.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The internet has evolved rapidly over the years, with each phase introducing groundbreaking innovations, from the rise of the Internet of Things (IoT) to the widespread adoption of cloud computing and the advancement of artificial intelligence (AI). Now, we are entering the next major phase of this evolution: **Web 4.0.**
● **Web 4.0 also known as the Symbiotic web.** It will aim at the **interaction between humans and** **machines in symbiosis.** Humans and machines will have a symbiotic relationship, where machines will be capable of learning from human interactions and providing insights that can assist in decision-making. The symbiotic web will facilitate the development of autonomous, proactive, content-exploring systems. These systems will be driven by self-learning, collaborative and content-generating agents based on fully matured semantics. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Web 4.0 services** will enable the **seamless integration of hardware, software and data.** Through these services, users will be able to connect to a wide variety of data sources, process that data in realtime and generate insights for informed decision-making.
● The services will also **facilitate the development of personalized experiences.** For example, **users will be able to access tailored content based on their interests and preferences.** This will enable users to be more engaged with the web, as they will be able to find content that is relevant to them more quickly and easily. **So, statement III is correct.**
● Web 4.0 services will also enable the **development of virtual reality (VR) and augmented reality** **(AR) applications.** These applications will enable users to interact with content in a three-dimensional space. This will open up new possibilities for content creation, and delivery and will revolutionize user experience. **So, statement II is correct.**
● Web 4.0 services will enable the development of machine-learning algorithms. These algorithms will enable machines to learn from data, generate insights and make decisions autonomously. This will enable machines to become smarter and more capable over time. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement-I:**
Splashdown in the ocean is preferred over land-
based landing for spacecraft returning to Earth.
**Statement-II:**
Water offers a greater cushioning effect due to its
high density and low viscosity.
**Statement-III:**
Oceans provide vast open areas that reduce the
risk of collision during landing.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of
the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement II and Statement III are
correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement II and Statement III are
correct, but only one of them explains
Statement I
options.c:
Only one of the Statements II and III is
correct and that explains Statement I
options.d:
Neither Statement II nor Statement III is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Splashdown** is a spacecraft landing technique where the **capsule touches down in the ocean,** slowed by parachutes and supported by flotation devices. Pioneered by NASA’s Apollo missions, it is still used by modern capsules like **SpaceX’s Crew Dragon** and soon, India’s Gaganyaan module. After reentry, the capsule floats upright until recovery teams arrive by ship or helicopter. **So, statement I is** **correct.**
● On re-entering the atmosphere at speeds over 27,000 km per hour, a spacecraft **decelerates rapidly** **due to friction,** then deploys parachutes to slow further. Even at **final descent speeds of 25–30 km** **per hour**, landing on hard ground could damage the capsule and injure the crew. The **high density** **and low viscosity of water act as a natural cushion, absorbing impact energy and reducing** **shock. So, statement II is correct.**
● Land landings require carefully chosen, **obstacle-free zones and sophisticated systems like** **landing legs or retro-propulsion** to touch down safely. By contrast, **vast ocean areas** mean small deviations from the planned path cause no danger. Capsules are built to float, with conical shapes and rounded hulls that stabilise them like a ship’s hull. **So, statement III is correct.**
● Ocean splashdowns avoid costly infrastructure like runways, landing pads, or airbags. Water provides natural shock absorption, so capsules do not need heavy landing legs or complex braking rockets. This makes splashdowns not only safer but also more cost-effective for current and future human spaceflight missions. **So, both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, after spending 18 days on the International Space Station and orbiting around the Earth 288 times, Indian astronaut Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla and his three colleagues on the Axiom-4 mission splashed down in the Pacific Ocean.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Space mission
in the news
Launched by
I. - TRACERS European Space Agency
II. - Proba-3 National Aeronautics and Space Administration
III. - LUPEX Indian Space Research Organisation
How many of the pairs given above are correctly
matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **NASA's TRACERS mission**, or the Tandem Reconnection and Cusp Electrodynamics Reconnaissance
Satellites, will fly in low Earth orbit through the polar cusps, funnel-shaped holes in the magnetic field, to
**study magnetic reconnection and its effects in Earth's atmosphere.** Magnetic reconnection
occurs when activity from the Sun interacts with Earth’s magnetic field. By understanding this process,
scientists will be able to better understand and prepare for the impacts of solar activity on Earth. **So,**
**pair I is not correctly matched.**
● **Proba-3** is an **In-Orbit Demonstration (IOD) mission** of the **European Space Agency**. The
mission goal is to **demonstrate precise formation flying**. It consists of two spacecraft, viz. the
Coronagraph Spacecraft (CSC) and the Occulter Spacecraft (OSC) and it will be launched together in a
stacked configuration. Launch is planned from First Launch Pad (FLP), Satish Dhawan Space Centre
(SDSC-SHAR), Sriharikota. **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
● The **LUPEX** project is an initiative aimed at **exploring the Moon for water** and other resources and
gaining expertise in **exploring the surface of the Moon.** The LUPEX project is an international
cooperative project, with **Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA)** in charge of the lunar
rover and the Indian Space Research Organisation (**ISRO)** responsible for the lander that will carry the
rover; observation instruments from NASA and the European Space Agency (ESA) will also be mounted
on the rover. **So, pair III is correctly matched.**
**So, only one of the pairs given above is correctly matched**
**Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
The terms shadow bands, diamond ring effect
and baily's beads” are associated with which of
the following astronomical events?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A **total solar eclipse** happens when the **Moon passes** **between the Sun and Earth,** completely blocking the face of the Sun. People located in the center of the Moon’s shadow when it hits Earth will experience a total eclipse. The sky will darken, as if it were dawn or dusk.
● **Baily's Beads** are a phenomenon observed just before and after totality during a total solar eclipse. As the **Moon** moves in front of the **Sun**, the rugged lunar landscape with its valleys, mountains and craters allows beads of sunlight to shine through in some places but not in others. This creates a series of bright spots or "beads" around the edge of the Moon, known as **Baily's beads**. The effect is named after English astronomer **Francis Baily**, who described it in 1836, though it was first recorded by Edmond Halley in 1715.
● The **Diamond Ring Effect** occurs when only one bright bead of sunlight remains visible through a lunar valley, while the rest of the Sun is obscured by the Moon. This single brilliant point, combined with the faint glow of the Sun’s corona around the lunar disk, resembles a sparkling **diamond ring**. The **diamond ring** is visible for just a few seconds before totality and again as totality ends, marking the dramatic entrance and exit of the full eclipse.
● **Shadow bands** are faint, wavy lines of alternating light and dark that can be seen sweeping across the ground or buildings just before and after totality during a **solar eclipse**. These bands are produced by **atmospheric turbulence**, which refracts and distorts the last thin sliver of sunlight when the **solar disk is 99% covered** by the Moon. The chaotic motion of air in the atmosphere bends the sunlight, creating moving bands of light and shadow that typically measure **1–2 inches (2.5–5 cm) wide** and travel at about **10 feet (3 meters) per second**. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the world witnessed a historic total solar eclipse, one of the longest of the 21st century.

questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
fermions:
I. - They are fundamental particles that are
defined by having integer spin values.
II. 	 They can occupy the same location in space
with the same spin.
III. 	 Quarks and leptons are the two basic types of
fermions.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Fermions** are fundamental particles of matter described by the **Standard Model of Particle** **Physics**, which is the most widely accepted theory explaining the universe's basic building blocks and their interactions.
● The Standard Model classifies all fundamental particles as either **fermions** or **bosons** based on their quantum mechanical property called **spin**.
● **Fermions** are **fundamental particles** that are defined by having **half-integer spin values** (such as **1/2, 3/2, 5/2**, etc.). This property sets them apart from **bosons**, which have **integer spin values** (like 0, 1, 2, ...). **Spin** is an **intrinsic quantum mechanical property** of particles and for **fermions**, it is always a **half-integer**. This characteristic plays a crucial role in their behaviour and in the formation of matter, as it leads to phenomena like the **Pauli Exclusion Principle**. **So, statement I is not** **correct.**
● **Identical fermions,** such as two electrons, **cannot occupy the same location in space** with the same quantum number. They cannot both spin in the same direction, but they can spin in opposite directions. Bosons, on the other hand, are exempt from this principle. This means **two bosons may** **occupy the same location in space with the same spin**. This is true even for composite bosons, such as helium. Several bosons in the same quantum state can collect into what is known as a **“Bose-** **Einstein Condensate.”** These Bose-Einstein condensates can be found in superfluid helium and scientists believe they also exist in neutron stars. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● In the Standard Model of particle physics, **quarks and leptons are the two fundamental types of** **fermions**. There are **twelve fermions,** **six quarks** and **six leptons**. **So, statement III is correct.**
○ **Quarks** come in six "flavours" (up, down, charm, strange, top, bottom) and three "colours" (a property related to the strong force). Quarks combine to form composite particles called **hadrons**:
■ **Baryons** (like protons and neutrons) are made of
three quarks. ■ **Mesons** are made of a quark and an antiquark
(mesons are actually bosons because of their integer spin). ■ Quarks interact via the **strong force** (carried by
gluons) and can also change type through the **weak force** (via W and Z bosons). ○ **Leptons** are fundamental particles that do **not** possess colour charge, so they do **not** interact via the strong force. There are six leptons:
■ Three **charged leptons**: electron, muon, tauon. ■ Three **neutrinos**: electron neutrino, muon
neutrino, tau neutrino. ■ Leptons generally do **not** form composite
particles and do not reside in the nucleus. **Neutrinos** are extremely abundant, have mass, and can change type through **neutrino oscillation**. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Earth Observation Satellites are used to monitor
which of the following?
I. - Sea surface temperature
II. 	 Different types of celestial bodies
III. 	 Volcanic activity
IV. 	 Amount of particulate matter in the air
V. 	 Forest height
Select the correct answer using the code given
below:

options.b: I, III, IV and V only
options.d: I, II, III, IV and V

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer:** **b**
**Explanation:**
● **Earth Observation Satellites (EOSs)** are spacecraft that are placed in a **temporary or permanent** **orbit around the Earth.** These satellites are equipped with sensors, mostly image-capturing ones, to collect data on the atmosphere and surface of the Earth and help in monitoring weather events, land-use patterns, vegetation cover, ice cover, oceanic patterns and climate change events.
● Earth observation satellites can carry a variety of sensors that are broadly classified as either **passive or** **active sensors** and **optical or microwave sensors.** Passive sensors are usually spectrometers and radiometers that measure reflected solar radiation, whereas active sensors produce pulses of energy and measure the reflected radiation.
● Passive optical sensors include spectrophotometers that measure **visible light, infrared light and** **thermal infrared emissions.** Visible and infrared light sensors record information on the Earth’s surface, such as landforms, the distribution of urban areas and vegetation patterns. Thermal infrared emissions are used to monitor land temperatures, **sea surface temperatures,** **volcanic activity** and fires. **So, points I and III are correct.**
● **LiDaR (Light Detection and Ranging)** sensors are active sensors that emit light from a laser and can be used to **measure forest height and the amount of particulate matter in the air.** Active microwave sensors, such as the synthetic aperture radar have been used to study Antarctic icebergs and track oil spills. **So, points IV and V are correct.**
● **Astronomy satellites** are used to **investigate different types of celestial bodies** and phenomena in space, from the creation of star and planetary surface maps and taking images of the planets in our solar system to the study of black holes. **So, point II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**

In how many of the above rows is the given information correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● An **artificial satellite** is any man-made object that has been placed into orbit by means of rockets. These spacecraft are equipped with sensitive instruments and cameras for studying Earth and other planets, aiding communication and even observing the distant universe.
● **Low Earth Orbit (LEO) Satellites:** These satellites are moving at an altitude of roughly **160–1,500** **kilometers** above the Earth’s surface. They have a short orbital period, between 90 and 120 minutes, meaning they can travel around the planet up to 16 times a day. This makes them particularly well-suited to all types of **remote sensing, high-resolution earth observation** and scientific research, as their short orbital period makes it possible to acquire and transmit satellite imagery data in real-time. **So, row** **I is correctly matched.**
● **Medium Earth Orbit (MEO) Satellites:** A Medium Earth type of orbit is located between low Earth and geostationary orbits, typically at an altitude of **about 5,000 to 20,000 kilometers.** Positioning and **navigation services, like the Global Positioning System (GPS),** extensively use MEO-type satellites. Recently, High-throughput satellite (HTS) MEO constellations have been put into operation to enable low-latency data communication to service providers, commercial and government organisations. **So, row II is correctly matched.**
● **Geostationary Orbit (GEO) Satellites:** Spacecraft in geostationary Earth orbit are **positioned** **35,786 kilometers above Earth’s surface,** precisely over the equator. Three evenly spaced machines in GEO can give nearly worldwide coverage thanks to the huge area they cover on Earth. This type of satellite is used for **always-on communication services like TV and phones.** Also, this type can be used in meteorology to keep an eye on the weather in particular regions and track the development of local patterns. **So, row III is not correctly matched.**
● **Sun-Synchronous Orbit (SSO) Satellites:** This type of satellite goes from north to south across the polar regions at an altitude of **600 to 800 km above the Earth.** The orbital inclination and altitude of SSO spacecraft are calibrated so that they always cross any given location at precisely the same local solar time. Thus, the lighting conditions are consistent for imaging, making this type of satellite ideal for **Earth observation and environmental monitoring. So, row IV is not correctly matched.** **So, in only two of the above rows is the given information correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:** Black holes are often formed when massive stars collapse under their own gravity.
**Statement II:** Black holes sustain themselves by fusing hydrogen into helium in their core.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does **not** explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is **not** correct
options.d: Statement I is **not** correct but Statement II is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Black holes** are some of the universe’s most **enigmatic entities**. Far from being empty “holes,” they are vast amounts of matter squeezed into unbelievably small volumes. This extreme density creates a gravitational pull so powerful near the **event horizon**—the threshold surrounding the black hole—that **nothing, not even light, can escape.** Unlike a solid surface on Earth or the Sun, the event horizon is an invisible boundary that encloses all the black hole’s mass.
● At the heart of a black hole lies what’s called a **“singularity”** — a point where our laws of **physics** **break down and gravity becomes infinite.**
● **Formation of Black Holes:** Black holes commonly form from the **gravitational collapse of** **massive stars**—typically those with a **mass more than eight times that of the Sun.** During their active lifetime, **these stars sustain themselves by** **fusing hydrogen into helium in their cores**. This nuclear fusion generates outward thermal pressure, which balances the inward pull of gravity, maintaining the star's structural stability.  However, once the **nuclear fuel is exhausted,** fusion reactions cease and the outward pressure diminishes. Gravity then dominates, causing the stellar core to **collapse rapidly under its own immense weight. So, statement I is correct and II is not** **correct.**
● If the remnant core is sufficiently massive—exceeding approximately three solar masses—even the neutron degeneracy pressure (a quantum mechanical force that resists compression of neutrons) fails to halt the collapse. In such a case, the **core continues to compress into a singularity,** surrounded by an event horizon and a black hole is formed. **So, Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements about the launch vehicles of India:
**Statement I:** Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) has the highest success rate in space launches for the ISRO.
**Statement II:** PSLV is a three-stage vehicle with the third stage called Cryogenic Upper Stage.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does **not** explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is **not** correct
options.d: Statement I is **not** correct but Statement II is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● India has three active operational launch vehicles: **Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV),** **Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV) and Geosynchronous Satellite Launch** **Vehicle Mk-III (LVM3)**.
● **PSLV is designed mainly to deliver the “earth-observation” or “remote-sensing”** satellites **to Sun-Synchronous circular polar orbits** of 600-900 Km altitude. However, PSLV has been a versatile launch vehicle deployed for launching all three types of payloads viz. Earth Observation, Geostationary and Navigation. It has the highest success rate and is considered the **workhorse of ISRO**. **So, statement I is correct.**
○ **The remote sensing satellites** orbit the Earth from pole to pole (at about 98 degrees orbitalplane inclination). An orbit is called sun-synchronous when the angle between the line joining the centre of the Earth and the satellite and the Sun is constant throughout the orbit. ○ Due to their sun-synchronism nature, these orbits are also referred to as **“Low Earth Orbit** **(LEO)**” which enables the onboard camera to take images of the Earth under the same sunillumination conditions during each of the repeated visits, the satellite makes over the same area on the ground, thus making the satellite useful for earth resources monitoring. ○ Apart from launching the remote sensing satellites to Sun-synchronous polar orbits, the PSLV is also used to launch the satellites of lower lift-off mass of up to about 1400 Kg to the elliptical **Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit** (**GTO**). **So, statement II is not correct.** ○ **PSLV is a four-stage launch vehicle** with **first and third stages using solid rocket** **motors and second and fourth stages using liquid rocket engines.** It also uses strap-on motors to augment the thrust provided by the first stage. **So, statement II is not correct.** ○ Depending on the number of these strap-on boosters, the PSLV is classified into its various versions, like the core-alone version (PSLV-CA), PSLV-G or PSLV-XL variants. **So, Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**About Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle:**
● **The GSLV is designed mainly to deliver the communication satellites** to the highly elliptical (typically 250 x 36000 Km) **Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit** **(GTO).** The satellite in GTO is further raised to its final destination, viz., Geosynchronous Earth orbit (GEO) of about 36000 Km altitude (and zero deg inclination on the equatorial plane) by firing its in-built onboard engines.
● Due to their geo-synchronous nature, the satellites in these orbits appear to remain permanently fixed in the same position in the sky, as viewed from a particular location on Earth, thus avoiding the need for a tracking ground antenna and are hence useful for communication applications.
● **GSLV MK-II is a three-stage vehicle with the first stage using solid rocket** **motors, the second stage using Liquid fuel and the third stage, called** **Cryogenic Upper Stage.**
● LVM3 is a three-stage launch vehicle consisting of two solid propellant S200 strapons and core stages comprising L110 liquid stage, C25 cryogenic stage. The LVM3 is the next generation launch vehicle capable of launching 4 tonne class of communication satellites and a 10-tonne class of payloads to LEOs.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to hypersonic weapons:
I. - Hypersonic Glide Vehicles (HGVs) are launched from a rocket, whereas Hypersonic Cruise Missiles (HCMs) are powered throughout their flight via air-breathing engines.
II. 	 Unlike ballistic missiles, HGVs do not follow a parabolic trajectory to their target.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **A hypersonic weapon** refers to one which travels faster than Mach 5, or five times the speed of sound (330 m/s).
● **There are two types: Hypersonic Glide Vehicles (HGV)** are launched from a rocket, similar to regular ballistic missiles, before gliding to a target, whereas **Hypersonic Cruise Missiles (HCM)** are powered throughout their flight via air-breathing engines called Scramjets, after acquiring their target. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **The manoeuvrability** of the hypersonic missile is what sets it apart from a ballistic missile, as the latter follows a set course or a ballistic trajectory. **Thus, unlike ballistic missiles, hypersonic missiles do** **not follow a parabolic ballistic trajectory** and can be manoeuvred to the intended target. **So,** **statement II is correct.**
● The report states that ground-based radars or terrestrial radars cannot detect hypersonic missiles until late in the flight of the weapon. This delayed detection makes it difficult for the responders to the missile attack to assess their options and to attempt to intercept the missile. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText: ‘Akashteer’, recently in the news, is:

options.a: A surface-to-surface tactical ballistic missile system
options.c: An indigenous loitering munition for precision strike
options.d: An automated Air Defence Control and Reporting System

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Akashteer System** is a cutting-edge **Air Defence Control and Reporting System** developed by **Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL)** for the Indian Army. It seamlessly integrates radars, sensors and communication technologies to deliver **real-time situational awareness and streamline** **command and control.**
● It is a system designed to automate the **detection, tracking and engagement of enemy aircraft,** **drones and missiles.** It integrates various radar systems, sensors and communication technologies into a single operational framework. Akashteer gathers data from multiple sources, processes it and allows for automated, real-time engagement decisions. It is part of the broader **C4ISR (Command, Control,** **Communications, Computers, Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance) framework,** working in coordination with other systems. The system is vehicle-based, which makes it mobile and easier to handle in a hostile environment. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Akashteer system played a crucial role in strengthening India’s air defence during the military standoff with Pakistan.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: IT, Computers, Space, NanoTech, BioTech, Robotics, Defence, Nuclear, General Science
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. - India ranks second in the world after China in producing graduates in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM).
II. 	 India’s expenditure on Research and Development (R&D), as a percentage of GDP, is less than all other founding members of BRICS.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● India has a large pool of **Scientific, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM)** talent, producing the **second-highest number of STEM graduates globally after China.** This availability of skilled people is demonstrated by the fact that **approximately 75% of the world's top 500** **multinational corporations** have established development centres in India. With such a rich talent base, India has the potential to greatly improve its technological capabilities and become one of the world's top three science and technology (S&T) nations. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Despite this potential, India now ranks 39th on the Global Innovation Index, with China in 11th. One of the primary causes of this disparity is a **lack of investment in Research and Development (R&D).** In 2022, **India spent only 0.65% of its GDP on R&D**, far less than other BRICS nations **like Brazil** **(1.15%), Russia (0.94%) and especially China (2.43%).** This figure is even more modest when compared to advanced S&T-driven economies such as the United States, Japan, Israel and South Korea. **So, statement II is correct.**
● As a lower-middle-income country, India still has a considerable journey ahead before it reaches its full productivity potential. Bridging the investment gap in R&D and fostering innovation are critical steps toward achieving global leadership in science and technology. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: IT, Computers, Space, NanoTech, BioTech, Robotics, Defence, Nuclear, General Science
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Term sometimes seen in the news Context
I. - VERVE‑102 Base‑editing gene therapy
II. - Willow chip III Quantum computing
III. - ALICE experiment Dark matter
IV. - LUX-ZEPLIN (LZ) experiment Particle physics
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **VERVE-102** uses a modified version of CRISPR, the famous gene-editing system. It uses **"base** **editing,"** which swaps out just one letter in DNA's code, thus sidestepping the danger of a doublestranded break. Like classic CRISPR, the base editor still includes a "guide" molecule to fix its aim at the correct gene, and from there, an enzyme tweaks just one letter in DNA's code.
○ It **targets a gene called PCSK9**, which controls the number of **Low-Density Lipoprotein** **(LDL)** receptors on the surfaces of cells. The quantity of these receptors dictates how quickly LDL gets cleared from the blood. When PCSK9 is too active, as it is in the genetic disease FH, it breaks down LDL receptors before they can make it to the cell surface, thus causing LDL to accumulate in the bloodstream instead. **So, pair I is correctly matched.** ○ It is being tested in people with Familial Hypercholesterolemia (FH), an inherited condition that raises the levels of **Low-Density Lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol** (bad cholesterol) in the blood.
● Recently, Google has developed a state-of-the-art **quantum computing chip called Willow** that solved in **under five minutes a computation so complex,** it would have taken a supercomputer around 10 septillion (1025) years to complete. The Willow chip is a major step on a journey that began over 10 years ago. **So, pair II is correctly matched.**
○ The components of the chip include single and two-qubit gates, qubit reset and readout that have been engineered and integrated to ensure that there is no lag between any two components, as that may adversely impact system performance.
● Bose Institute, Kolkata, is the only Autonomous Institute under the Department of Science and Technology, Government of India, working in **the Large Ion Collider Experiment** (ALICE) at CERN, along with many other collaborators in India.
○ For part of each year, the LHC provides collisions between lead ions, recreating in laboratory conditions similar to those just after the Big Bang. Under these extreme conditions, protons and neutrons "melt", freeing the quarks from their bonds with the gluons. This is quark-gluon plasma. The existence of such a phase and its properties are key issues in the theory of **Quantum** **Chromodynamics** (QCD), for understanding the phenomenon of confinement and for a physics problem called **chiral-symmetry restoration.** ○ The ALICE collaboration studies the **quark-gluon plasma** as it expands and cools, observing how it progressively gives rise to the particles that constitute the matter of our universe today. **So, pair III is not correctly matched.** ○ The $3 million Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics for 2025 is awarded to thousands of researchers from more than 70 countries representing four experimental collaborations at CERN’s Large Hadron Collider (LHC) – ATLAS, CMS, ALICE and LHCb. **Bose Institute,** **Kolkata,** is one of the winners.
● **LUX-ZEPLIN (LZ)** is a next-generation **dark matter experiment**, selected by the US Department of Energy (DOE) as one of the three **‘G2’ (for Generation 2)** dark matter experiments. The LZ collaboration consists of about 250 scientists in 39 institutions in the U.S., U.K., Portugal, Switzerland, South Korea and Australia. The name LZ stems from the merger of two previous dark matter detection experiments: LUX (Large Underground Xenon) and ZEPLIN (ZonEd Proportional scintillation in Liquid Noble gases). **So, pair IV is not correctly matched.** **So, only two of the pairs given above are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. - Cloud computing can operate independently of IoT or edge computing devices.
II. 	 Internet of Things (IoT) systems can function without relying on either edge computing or cloud infrastructure.
III. 	 Edge computing necessarily requires cloud connectivity to process and store data.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **A cloud** is an IT environment that abstracts, pools and shares IT resources across a network. **The edge** refers to devices at or near the physical location of either the user or the source of the data. **Cloud** **computing** is the act of running workloads within clouds, while **edge computing** is the act of running workloads on edge devices.
● **Internet of Things (IoT)** refers to any system of physical devices that receive and transfer data over wireless networks with limited human intervention. Cloud, edge and IoT can all be connected. But they do not have to be connected.
○ **Clouds can exist without the Internet of Things (IoT) or edge devices**. **So, statement** **I is correct.** ○ While many IoT systems utilise cloud or edge computing, it's **not a requirement for all IoT** **systems to function**. Some IoT devices can operate independently, processing data locally without relying on external infrastructure. **So, statement II is correct.** ○ **Edge computing bypasses centralised cloud and datacenter locations** to allow companies to process data more quickly and reliably, in real time or close to it. Some edge devices connect to a cloud or private datacenter, others edge devices only connect to similarly central locations intermittently and others never connect to anything at all. But edge computing, when used as part of manufacturing, mining, processing, or shipping operations, rarely exists without IoT. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
With regard to Radio Frequency Identification
(RFID) and Barcode technologies, consider the
following statements:
I. - While RFID uses radio waves for
communication, barcodes rely on optical
scanning using light.
II. 	 Unlike barcodes, RFID systems do not
require a line of sight between the reader and
the tag.
III. 	 RFID tags can function without an internal
power source, whereas barcode labels have
no electronic components.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Radio frequency identification (RFID)** is a type of wireless communication technology that uses a **scanning antenna, a transceiver and a transponder.** An RFID reader is made up of the antenna and transceiver. The transponder is known as an RFID tag or smart label. Unique tags are attached to products and assets throughout the supply chain so that users can find the items with RFID readers.
● **A barcode** is a printed series of parallel bars or lines of varying width used for entering data into a computer system. The bars represent the **binary digits zero and one**, which represent the digits zero to nine processed by a digital computer. These barcodes are scanned **using special optical scanners** known as barcode readers. **It uses light to read the black and white pattern** printed on the sticky tag. However, **RFID uses radio waves to communicate data** from RFID chips to readers that do not require a line of sight to obtain the data. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Barcode** scanners rely on **line-of-sight**, so users must physically move products if necessary to find the barcode and scan it. **RFID scanners**, on the other hand, are a near-field technology, which allows the scanner to read the tags within a range and **without a direct line-of-sight**. **So, statement II is** **correct.**
● There are different types of RFID tags: Passive tags, semi-passive tags and active tags. **Active RFIDs use** **their own power source, mostly batteries.** Active tags can ping information every few seconds, like beacons, or they can get activated when a reader is in the proximity. Passive RFIDs, on the other hand, are activated through the reader using the electromagnetic energy it transmits. This is enough power for the tag to transmit information back to the reader. **Thus, RFID tags can operate without an** **internal power source. Barcode labels, on the other hand, are purely passive and rely on** **optical scanning, containing no electronic components. So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to stablecoins, consider the
following statements:
I. - The value of stablecoins is typically pegged
to a reserve asset such as fiat currency or
gold.
II. 	 Stablecoins guarantee a stable value in
secondary markets.
III. 	 Stablecoins are restricted from being used in
purchasing goods or services.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Stablecoins** are a category of cryptocurrency designed to **minimise price volatility by pegging** **their value to an external reference asset**, such as a **fiat currency** (e.g., U.S. dollar), a **commodity** **(e.g., gold),** or even another financial instrument. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Stablecoins** offer a more stable alternative to highly volatile cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin. They can be utilised in various blockchain-based financial services and **can even be used to pay for goods and** **services. So, statement III is not correct.**
● thereby making them more practical for everyday transactions and blockchain-based financial services.
● However, despite the term **"stablecoin," price stability is not guaranteed in secondary markets.** Additionally, the adequacy of the reserve backing (if any) may not always be sufficient to fulfil all redemption requests. **So, statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The US House of Representatives recently passed the Genesis Act, a bill to regulate US dollarpegged stablecoins.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
With reference to types of internet connectivity,
consider the following statements:
I. - Satellite-based internet experiences higher
latency than fibre-optic broadband.
II. 	 Fibre-optic broadband is more suitable for
real-time applications than satellite internet.
III. 	 Unlike satellite internet, fibre-optic
broadband is largely confined to urban and
semi-urban regions.
IV. 	 Satellite internet is largely unaffected by
local weather conditions than fibre-optic
broadband.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Both satellite internet and broadband offer internet access, but their underlying technology and performance vary significantly.
● **Satellite internet** operates by transmitting data between a satellite orbiting the Earth and a dish installed at the user’s location, while **Broadband** is a high-speed internet connection delivered via **fiber-** **optic cables**, DSL (Digital Subscriber Line), or coaxial cable.
● Key differences between satellite internet and broadband internet are as follows: ○ **Speed and Performance:** Broadband, especially fibre-optic, offers much higher data transfer speeds, often reaching 1 Gbps or more, making it ideal for high-bandwidth applications such as video streaming, cloud computing and online gaming. Satellite Internet typically provides speeds ranging from 25 to 100 Mbps, sufficient for **basic browsing and video calls, but not optimal** **for data-intensive or latency-sensitive tasks.** ○ **Latency:** Latency refers to the delay in data transmission. **Satellite Internet** suffers from **high** **latency (600 ms or more)** due to the **long distance signals** must travel to geostationary satellites and back. Broadband, especially fibre-optic, **provides low latency (<20 ms),** enabling **real-time interactions like online gaming, video conferencing and VoIP calls. So,** **statements I and II are correct.** ○ **Data Caps and Usage:** Satellite connections often come with data caps, limiting monthly usage and reducing speeds after a certain threshold. Most broadband plans, especially in urban areas, offer unlimited data, supporting continuous high-speed access without throttling. ○ **Coverage and Availability:** Satellite Internet offers **global coverage,** including remote, rural, mountainous and maritime regions, where terrestrial infrastructure is difficult or economically unviable to deploy. Broadband is generally **limited to urban and suburban areas,** where infrastructure like fibre cables and DSL lines are physically present. **So, statement III is** **correct.** ○ **Weather Dependency:** Satellite signals are **affected by local weather conditions,** including rain, storms, or heavy clouds—a phenomenon known as rain fade, which can degrade signal quality or cause outages. Broadband connections, especially underground fibre, are **largely** **immune to weather disruptions, offering more stable performance. So, statement** **IV is not correct.** ○ **Cost Considerations:** Satellite internet typically involves higher installation and operational costs, including satellite dishes and higher monthly charges. Broadband offers more cost-effective high-speed connectivity, especially in densely populated areas where infrastructure costs are shared across users. **So, only three of the above statements are correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:**
T-cells can be used for the treatment of cancers.
**Statement II:**
T-cells can be genetically engineered to express
receptors that specifically target antigens on cells.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of
the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
but Statement II does **not** explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is **not**
correct
options.d:
Statement I is **not** correct but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **CAR-T cell therapy** (Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-cell therapy) is a cutting-edge form of immunotherapy that harnesses the **power of the body's own immune system** to combat cancer. This therapy represents a significant advancement in personalised medicine and cancer treatment.
● The immune system, the body's natural defence mechanism, relies heavily on **White Blood Cells** **(WBCs)** to identify and eliminate harmful pathogens and abnormal cells. Among these, **T-cells** play a central role in mounting a targeted immune response.
● In CAR-T cell therapy, a patient’s **T-cells are extracted and genetically modified** in a laboratory to **produce special receptors on their surface known as chimeric antigen receptors (CARs).** These receptors are engineered to recognise specific antigens present on cancer cells. **So, statements** **I and II are correct.**
● Once infused back into the patient, these modified T-cells can: ○ Precisely bind to cancer cells ○ Trigger a cytotoxic immune response ○ Eliminate malignant cells with high specificity.
● Other cells, like B cells or natural killer cells, also play roles in immunity but don’t have the same adaptability and memory capabilities as T cells, making them less effective for the persistent and targeted action required in CAR-T cell therapy. **So, both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, a study published in the Lancet Haematology has reported a 73% response rate for CAR T-cell therapy among patients with certain blood cancers.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR):
I. - It occurs when microorganisms evolve mechanisms that protect them from the effects of antimicrobials.
II. 	 It is limited to bacteria and does not affect treatment outcomes for viral or fungal infections.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR)** is a condition in which **bacteria, viruses, fungi and parasites** no longer respond to antimicrobial medicines, which include **antibiotics, antivirals, antifungals and** **antiparasitics.** As a result, infections become difficult or impossible to treat, increasing the risk of disease spread, severe illness, disability and death. It is the result of the evolution of microbes in a situation where there is a **misuse or overuse of antibiotics. So, statement II is not correct.**
● **Factors contributing to the spread of Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR):** ○ **Excessive use of Antimicrobial Drugs:** The irrational, **excessive use of antimicrobial** **drugs** is the key reason for the rise of AMR as it leads to the creation of resistant or extremely resistant superbugs, which can circulate in hospitals, through drinking water, or sewers. **So,** **statement I is correct.** ○ **Lack of Hygiene Practices:** Another reason for the increase in antimicrobial resistance (AMR) is the inadequate hygiene practices and the lack of proper infection control in hospitals and clinics,
which facilitate the spread of resistant bacteria.
○ **Pharmaceutical waste:** Pharmaceutical waste from antibiotic manufacturing can facilitate the emergence of new drug-resistant bacteria, which can spread globally and threaten our health. Controlling pollution from antibiotic production contributes to keeping these life-saving medicines effective for everyone. ○ **Unregulated Access to Antibiotics:** An area of great concern is the unregulated access to antibiotics in the **animal husbandry, dairy, and poultry sectors**. The regulation of antibiotic sales for non-human consumption generally goes unnoticed. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the WHO published its first-ever guidance on antibiotic pollution from manufacturing ahead of the UN General Assembly High-Level Meeting on antimicrobial resistance.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Type of Hydrogen Source/Process
I. - Grey Hydrogen Produced using electrolysis powered by solar or wind energy
II. - Blue Hydrogen Produced from fossil fuels with carbon capture and storage
III. - Green Hydrogen Produced from natural gas without carbon capture
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Hydrogen**, the most common element in nature, exists only in combination with other elements and has to be extracted from naturally occurring compounds like **water** (which is a combination of **two** **hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom**). Hydrogen is a clean molecule, but the process of extracting it is energy-intensive.
● **Types of Hydrogen: Based on Production Sources:** Hydrogen is not found freely in nature and must be produced through various processes. To distinguish between these methods, a colour-coded classification is used:
○ **Grey Hydrogen:** It is produced from **fossil fuels (mainly natural gas)** through processes like **Steam Methane Reforming (SMR).** This method emits significant amounts of carbon dioxide (CO₂) and constitutes the majority of global hydrogen production today. **So, pair I is** **not correctly matched.** ○ **Blue Hydrogen:** It is also **produced from fossil fuels,** but includes the use of **Carbon** **Capture and Storage (CCS)** technologies to trap and store CO₂ emissions, making it a relatively cleaner option than grey hydrogen. **So, pair II is correctly matched.** ○ **Green Hydrogen:** It is generated by using **electrolysers powered by renewable energy** **sources** (such as **solar or wind**) to split water into hydrogen and oxygen. This method is emission-free and represents the most sustainable form of hydrogen production. **So, pair III is** **not correctly matched.** **So, only one of the above pairs is correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: IT, Computers, Space, NanoTech, BioTech, Robotics, Defence, Nuclear, General Science
questions.questionText: "Ghost particles", sometimes seen in the news, refer to:

options.a: Hypothetical particles predicted by string theory that travel faster than light
options.b: Neutrinos, subatomic particles that interact very weakly with matter
options.c: Dark matter particles detected through gravitational lensing
options.d: Exotic antimatter particles created in particle accelerators

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer:** **b**
**Explanation:**
● In a significant advancement in **particle astrophysics,** scientists are deploying two large underwater telescopes beneath the **Mediterranean Sea** as part of the **KM3NeT (Cubic Kilometre Neutrino** **Telescope)** project. These cutting-edge instruments aim to detect elusive subatomic particles known as **high-energy neutrinos,** often referred to as **“ghost particles” due to their extremely weak** **interaction with matter.**
● The KM3NeT array comprises two distinct telescopes: ○ One will focus on detecting cosmic neutrinos originating from deep space.
○ The other is designed to observe atmospheric neutrinos, produced when cosmic rays interact with Earth’s atmosphere.
● This initiative is conceptually similar to the **IceCube Neutrino Observatory** located under the **Antarctic ice**. However, instead of being embedded in ice, KM3NeT operates from deep-sea locations, using the clarity and depth of the ocean water as a medium for detection. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**What Are Neutrinos?**
● Neutrinos are fundamental subatomic particles, first detected in 1959, although their existence was theoretically predicted in 1931 by physicist Wolfgang Pauli. These particles:
o are electrically neutral, hence do not interact via electromagnetic forces. o have an extremely small mass, much lighter than electrons. o belong to the family of leptons, one of the building blocks of matter.
● Neutrinos are nearly massless, electrically neutral subatomic particles that interact extremely weakly with matter.
● Their tendency not to interact very often with other particles makes detecting neutrinos very difficult.
**Relevance:** Recently, Chinese scientists placed special detectors deep in the South China Sea to explore the possibility of building a huge underwater observatory to find neutrinos.

questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. - Silicon
II. 	 Germanium
III. 	 Indium phosphide
IV. 	 Boron
V. 	 Gallium
How many of the above are used as raw materials in semiconductor chips?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A **semiconductor** is a material product with some of the properties of both insulators and conductors. Semiconductors are essential to the Information Age, forming the basis of all computer chips. The main **raw materials** for semiconductors include **silicon**, **germanium**, **phosphorus**, **boron**, **indium** **phosphide** and **gallium**.
● **Silicon** is the most widely used semiconductor material, sourced mainly from beach sand. It is processed into wafers and undergoes steps like **photolithography**, **ion implantation** and **etching** before metal circuits (aluminium, copper) are attached. The United States of America contributes about 12% of global semiconductor manufacturing. **So, point I is correct.**
● **Germanium** is used in chips for **solar cells**, **fiber optics** and **night-vision devices**. Most of it comes as a byproduct of coal or zinc processing, with China dominating global supply. **So, point II is correct.**
● **Indium phosphide** is used in electronics like **laser diodes and photonic integrated circuits** in the **optical telecommunications field**. Indium phosphide is a binary compound of phosphorus and indium. **So, point III is correct.**
● **Boron** is critical for making **computer chips**. Its main use is to bind different materials together, improving their efficacy. It can also cause insulators to be more conducive to electricity. Turkey is the largest producer of boron. It also improves the dielectric strength of polymers, which are part of sealing gaskets, capacitors and the insulation layers on wires. **So, point IV is correct.**
● **Gallium** is found in bauxite and zinc ores and used to make **gallium arsenide** chips, which are heatresistant and suited for high-frequency devices like satellites, LEDs, and radar. **So, point V is correct.**
● To manufacture **p-type** and **n-type** semiconductors, materials are **doped** with **impurities** like **boron** and **phosphorus**. **Boron** (from Turkey) is used to create p-type semiconductors and improve dielectric properties, while **phosphorus** is used for n-type doping by introducing extra electrons. **So, all five of the above are used as raw materials in semiconductor chips.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Hazardous, Radioactive, E-Waste, Heavy Metals, etc.
questions.questionText: The use of perfluorooctane sulfonate (PFOS) and perfluorooctanoic acid (PFOA) is viewed with apprehension around the world. These chemicals are primarily used as:

options.a: Fertilisers in high-yield genetically modified crop production
options.b: Additives in fire-fighting foams, stainresistant fabrics and non-stick cookware
options.c: Catalysts in green hydrogen fuel cells
options.d: Preservatives in pharmaceutical drugs and vaccines

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Perfluorooctane sulfonate (PFOS)** and **perfluorooctanoic acid (PFOA)** are part of the larger group of chemicals known as **per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS),** often referred to as **“forever chemicals”** due to their extreme resistance to environmental degradation. Since the 1950s, PFAS have been widely used in industrial and consumer products for their water-, grease and **stain-** **resistant** properties. These include **non-stick cookware (e.g., Teflon),** water-repellent fabrics, **firefighting foams**, paints and food packaging.
● PFOS and PFOA are highly persistent in soil and water and have been detected globally, even in remote regions like Arctic ice. They are also **bioaccumulative,** meaning they build up in the tissues of living organisms, including humans, where they have been associated with a range of health problems such as thyroid disorders, kidney disease, certain cancers and developmental issues.
● Due to these risks, PFOS and PFOA are now subject to bans and strict regulation under the **Stockholm** **Convention on Persistent Organic Pollutants.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**

questions.questionText:
Which of the following is/are an objective under India's National Quantum Mission?
I. - Development of quantum computers with 20–1000 qubits across various platforms
II. 	 Establishing satellite-based quantum communication between ground stations over 2000 km within India
III. 	 Setting up quantum-encrypted 5G communication infrastructure nationwide
IV.	 Advancing quantum sensing, atomic clocks and novel quantum materials
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The Union Cabinet approved the **National Quantum Mission (NQM),** aiming to seed, nurture and scale up **scientific and industrial R&D** and create a vibrant & innovative ecosystem in **Quantum** **Technology (QT).** This will accelerate QT-led economic growth, nurture the ecosystem in the country and make India one of the leading nations in the development of Quantum Technologies & Applications (QTA).
● **Objectives of the National Quantum Mission:** With the broader aim to harness quantum technologies in India to bolster sectors like communication, cryptography, and computing, the National Quantum Mission has outlined specific objectives to advance India's capabilities in the quantum realm:
○ **Quantum Computing Evolution:** Develop intermediate-scale quantum computers with **20-** **50 physical qubits** (3 years), 50-100 physical qubits (5 years), and **50-1000 physical qubits** (8 years) across platforms like superconducting and photonic technologies to advance computational capabilities. **So, point I is correct.** ○ **Satellite-Based Quantum Communication:** Establish satellite-enabled quantum-secured communication between **two ground stations over 2000 km within India** and extend this technology for long-distance secure quantum communication with other countries. **So, point II** **is correct.**
○ **Inter-City** **Quantum** **Key** **Distribution** **(QKD):** Implement quantum-secured communication spanning 2000 km using trusted nodes and wavelength division multiplexing (WDM) on existing optical fiber infrastructure, enhancing secure data transmission. ○ **Multi-Node Quantum Networks:** Develop a multi-node quantum network incorporating quantum memories, entanglement swapping and synchronised quantum repeaters at each node, enabling scalable and robust quantum communication (2-3 nodes). ○ **Advanced Quantum Sensing & Clocks:** Design **highly sensitive quantum devices,** including magnetometers with 1 femto-Tesla/sqrt(Hz) sensitivity in atomic systems and better than 1 pico-Tesla/sqrt(Hz) in Nitrogen Vacancy centers, gravity sensors with better than 100 nano-meter/second² sensitivity, and **atomic clocks with 10**⁻**¹****⁹** **fractional instability for** **precision timing,** navigation, and secure communication. ○ **Quantum Materials & Devices:** Develop and synthesise next-generation quantum materials such as superconductors, **novel semiconductor structures,** and topological materials for the fabrication of qubits, single-photon sources/detectors, entangled photon sources and quantum sensing/metrological devices for applications in computing and communication. **So, point IV is** **correct.**
● **Setting up a quantum-encrypted 5G communication** infrastructure nationwide is not explicitly mentioned in the objectives of the mission. **So, point III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**

questions.questionText:
With reference to sodium-ion batteries, consider the following statements with regard to their advantages over lithium-ion batteries:
I. - Sodium can be extracted from seawater at relatively lower costs than lithium.
II. 	 They can have a lower environmental impact compared to lithium-ion batteries.
III. 	 Unlike lithium-ion batteries, they can be stored at zero voltage.
IV. 	 They can operate efficiently across a wider temperature range than lithium-ion batteries.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Sodium-ion batteries** are emerging alternatives to lithium-ion batteries, using sodium ions for charge storage and transfer. They operate on similar principles but use sodium, which is a more abundant and cheaper element than lithium.
● **Sodium** is one of the **most abundant elements on Earth** and can be easily extracted from **common sources such as seawater and salt deposits.** In contrast, lithium is much rarer and is concentrated in limited geographic regions, mainly in countries like Chile, Argentina and Australia. Lithium extraction is an expensive and environmentally taxing process, involving significant water use and mining operations. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Sodium-ion batteries** have a more environmentally friendly profile because they **do not rely on** **scarce or toxic materials such as cobalt and nickel,** which are often used in lithium-ion batteries. The mining of these metals has been linked to serious **environmental degradation and human** **rights issues.** Sodium, on the other hand, is non-toxic and more sustainably sourced. Additionally, the production processes for sodium-ion batteries tend to have a lower overall carbon footprint. **So,** **statement II is correct.**
● **Lithium-ion batteries** must retain a certain **charge during transportation** because completely discharging them can lead to irreversible damage. However, this partially charged state **introduces fire** **and explosion risks,** especially if the battery is damaged or short-circuited. In contrast, **sodium-ion** **batteries can be fully discharged to zero volts** without compromising their structural integrity or future performance. This allows for safer and more flexible handling, storage and shipping. **So,** **statement III is correct.**
● Sodium-ion batteries typically **exhibit better thermal stability than lithium-ion batteries,** which means they can operate efficiently in both **high and low temperature environments.** Lithium-ion batteries are sensitive to temperature fluctuations, which can impact their performance, cycle life and safety. Overheating can lead to thermal runaway, while cold temperatures can significantly reduce their energy output. **So, statement IV is correct.** **So, all four of the above statements are correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText: The National Centre for Disease Informatics and Research (NCDIR) works under the aegis of:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **National Centre for Disease Informatics and Research (NCDIR)** is a permanent institute under the **Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR),** which functions as an autonomous body under the **Department of Health Research, Ministry of Health & Family Welfare,** Government of India. It is located in Bengaluru, Karnataka.
● NCDIR is a unique institution that integrates information science with health research. Its primary objective is to **develop and sustain a national research database on critical non-** **communicable diseases** such as cancer, diabetes, cardiovascular diseases (CVD) and stroke. By leveraging advances in electronic information technology and working through a national collaborative network, NCDIR undertakes aetiological, epidemiological, clinical, and control research to support datadriven policy and healthcare strategies.
● According to the study conducted by the National Centre for Disease Informatics and Research (NCDIR), India has the **second-highest number of adults with diabetes** in the world, and more than **one-fourth of adults have hypertension.** The study was undertaken to assess the “preparedness of public & private health facilities for management of diabetes & hypertension in 19 districts in India. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Public Primary Health Centres (PHCs) are better prepared for diabetes and hypertension care, a study undertaken by the National Centre for Disease Informatics and Research has found.
